heading: Civilian special agents of the Office of Special Investigations: authority to execute warrants and make arrests
label: 9377 Civilian special agents of the Office of Special Investigations: authority to execute warrants and make arrests
notes: Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 9027 of this title

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019

children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: a .—Authority
children.text: The Secretary of the Air Force may authorize any Department of the Air Force civilian employee described in subsection (b) to have the same authority to execute and serve warrants and other processes issued under the authority of the United States and to make arrests without a warrant as may be authorized under for special agents of the Defense Criminal Investigative Service.section 1585a of this title

children.heading: .—Agents To Have Authority
children.label: b .—Agents To Have Authority
children.text: Subsection (a) applies to any employee of the Department of the Air Force who is a special agent of the Air Force Office of Special Investigations (or a successor to that office) whose duties include conducting, supervising, or coordinating investigations of criminal activity in programs and operations of the Department of the Air Force.

children.heading: .—Guidelines for Exercise of Authority
children.label: c .—Guidelines for Exercise of Authority
children.text: The authority provided under subsection (a) shall be exercised in accordance with guidelines prescribed by the Secretary of the Air Force and approved by the Secretary of Defense and the Attorney General and any other applicable guidelines prescribed by the Secretary of the Air Force, the Secretary of Defense, or the Attorney General.
